perturbation.original_text: The equity trading desks execute buy and sell order based on instructions provided by affiliated advisers. The trading staff either places orders electronically or contacts brokers to place orders, find liquidity and seek price levels. Upon completion of a transaction, the transaction settlement group works with the broker and the account custodian to ensure timely and accurate exchange of securities and monies.
perturbation.changed_text: The equity trading desks will endeavor to execute buy and sell orders based on instructions provided by affiliated advisers. The trading staff may place orders electronically or contact brokers to place orders, find liquidity, and seek price levels. Upon completion of a transaction, the transaction settlement group will attempt to work with the broker and the account custodian to facilitate exchange of securities and monies.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'execute' to 'endeavor to execute', 'places' to 'may place', and 'ensure timely and accurate exchange' to 'facilitate exchange' introduces ambiguity regarding the obligation to fulfill trading instructions and settlement accuracy. This weakens the mandatory nature of the original language.
perturbation.law_explanation: SEC Rule 17Ad-22(b)(1) mandates that covered clearing agencies establish, implement, maintain and enforce written policies and procedures reasonably designed to ensure prompt and accurate clearance and settlement of securities transactions. By changing the language to 'endeavor to execute' and 'attempt to work with', the agreement creates ambiguity and weakens the guarantee of prompt and accurate settlement, potentially violating this rule.

perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces ambiguity regarding the obligation to fulfill trading instructions and settlement accuracy. The original text used mandatory language ('execute', 'places', 'ensure'), while the changed text uses weaker, discretionary language ('endeavor to execute', 'may place', 'facilitate'). According to the Law Explanation, SEC Rule 17Ad-22(b)(1) mandates that clearing agencies ensure prompt and accurate clearance and settlement of securities transactions. The scraped law snippet 1 is an eCFR link to 17 CFR 240.17ad-22, indicating the importance of this rule. The changes weaken the guarantee of prompt and accurate settlement, potentially violating this rule, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Based on this proprietary analysis, the analyst makes buy, sell or hold recommendations to the Adviser.
perturbation.changed_text: Based on this analysis, the analyst may occasionally offer buy, sell, or hold suggestions to the Adviser.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'proprietary analysis' to 'this analysis', 'makes recommendations' to 'may occasionally offer suggestions' significantly weakens the obligation and quality of the analyst's input, making the service less reliable and potentially breaching the fiduciary duty.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 206 of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 establishes the fiduciary duty of investment advisers to act in the best interest of their clients and to provide advice that is based on reasonable inquiry and analysis. By changing 'proprietary analysis' to 'this analysis' and weakening the recommendation to occasional suggestions, the amendment introduces ambiguity and diminishes the assurance that the advice is well-founded and in the client's best interest, potentially violating the fiduciary duty mandated by the Act.

Prohibited transactions by investment advisers It shall be unlawful for any investment adviser by use of the mails or any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, directly or indirectly- (1) to employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud any client or prospective client; (2) to engage in any transaction, practice, or course of business which
perturbation.justification: The modification from 'proprietary analysis' to 'this analysis' and 'makes recommendations' to 'may occasionally offer suggestions' weakens the analyst's obligation and the quality of their input. This creates an ambiguous legal obligation and diminishes the assurance that the advice is well-founded and in the client's best interest. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing 15 USC 80b-6, prohibits investment advisers from employing any 'device, scheme, or artifice to defraud any client'. By diminishing the reliability and quality of the analysis, the modification could be interpreted as a scheme to defraud, as the advice given might not be based on a thorough, proprietary analysis, potentially violating the fiduciary duty.

perturbation.original_text: Quantitative analysts develop and apply financial models designed to enable equity portfolio managers and fundamental analysts to screen potential and current investments, assess relative risk and enhance performance relative to benchmarks and peers.
perturbation.changed_text: Quantitative analysts might explore the use of financial models, possibly assisting portfolio managers in considering investments, with the goal of vaguely assessing risk and contributing to performance.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying 'develop and apply financial models' to 'might explore the use of financial models', 'enable...to screen' to 'possibly assisting...in considering', 'assess relative risk and enhance performance' to 'vaguely assessing risk and contributing to performance' introduces substantial ambiguity, diminishing the clarity and reliability of the quantitative analysis services.
perturbation.law_explanation: SEC Rule 206(4)-7 requires registered investment advisers to adopt and implement written policies and procedures reasonably designed to prevent violation of the Advisers Act. The ambiguity introduced in the modified text weakens the assurance that the quantitative analysis is conducted with sufficient rigor and reliability to adequately assess risk and performance, potentially undermining the effectiveness of the firm's compliance program and violating this rule.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 275.206(4)-7 - Compliance procedures and practices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 275.206(4)-7 - Compliance procedures and practices. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 275.206(4)-7 Compliance procedures and practices. If you are an investment adviser registered or required to be registered under section 203 of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80b-3 ), it shall be unlawful within the meaning of section 206 of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 80b-6 ) for you to provide investment advice to clients unless you: (a) Policies and procedures. Adopt and implement written policies and procedures reasonably designed to prevent violation, by you and your supervised persons, of the Act and the rules that the Commission has adopted under the Act; (b) Annual review. Review, no less frequently than annually, the adequacy of the policies and procedures established pursuant to this section and the effectiveness of their implementation; and (c) Chief compliance officer. Designate an individual (who is a supervised person) responsible for administering the policies and procedures that you adopt under paragraph (a) of this section. [ 68 FR 74730 , Dec. 24, 2003, as amended at 88 FR 63386 , Sept. 14, 2023; 89 FR 91253 , Nov. 19, 2024] Investment Advisers Act of 1940 CFR Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity that weakens the assurance that quantitative analysis will be conducted with sufficient rigor. SEC Rule 206(4)-7 requires registered investment advisers to "adopt and implement written policies and procedures reasonably designed to prevent violation...of the Act and the rules that the Commission has adopted under the Act". By changing the language to 'might explore the use of financial models, possibly assisting portfolio managers in considering investments, with the goal of vaguely assessing risk', the modified text reduces the likelihood that the policies and procedures will be effective in preventing violations, which is a contradiction.
